summary.intent: Refactor the Gossamer app's checkpoint routes to use localPath query params instead of scanning all repo DBs, fix missing planning messages in SessionDetail, rework the BranchLog screen to look like SessionDetail with pagination and lazy-loaded log events, and fix various UI bugs.
summary.outcome: All major refactors completed: repoForCheckpoint removed, localPath threading through server+frontend, BranchLog reimplemented with git-log-driven pagination (50 commits/page) and lazy-loaded log events per checkpoint, planning text folded into thinking blocks, breadcrumbs fixed, and diff line number sticky bug fixed.

repo: Claude Code writes two JSONL assistant events per API response for the same message: first text-only (planning), then tool-use-only with same message.id but different uuid. logEventsToEvents() needs to handle this or the planning text gets overwritten.
The branch-log endpoint uses git log with --format=%B%x1e to parse commit bodies, extracting Entire-Checkpoint: <id> trailers to map commits to checkpoint IDs without needing OID-based DB joins.
SQLite IN clauses hit a ~999 variable limit; large session/checkpoint sets cause PrismaClientKnownRequestError 'too many SQL variables'.
Each repo can have its own .db file; findRepo(localPath) maps path → dbPath. Direct localPath lookup is far more efficient than repoForCheckpoint which scanned all repo DBs.
diff2html sets position:sticky on .d2h-code-linenumber cells for horizontal scrolling; inside Mantine ScrollArea this causes them to behave like position:fixed and not scroll with content.
gitUserName comes from checkpointMetadata, not from the session or logEvent rows; EventItem falls back to 'You' when user prop is absent.

code.finding: logEventsToEvents() uses simple assignment for lastAssistantText, so a post-tool-use text block overwrites the earlier planning text. Fix: detect new text after tool uses in same turn and fold old text into lastAssistantThinking.

mapLogEvent parameter type must be derived from the actual findMany call with includes (LogEventWithIncludes = Awaited<ReturnType<typeof db.logEvent.findMany<{ include: typeof LOG_EVENT_INCLUDE }>>>[number]) — using bare findMany return type omits relation fields like systemData, hookProgress.
Override position:sticky to position:static on .d2h-code-linenumber and .d2h-code-side-linenumber to fix scroll anchoring inside Mantine ScrollArea.
Lazy-load pattern: each EntryDetail component fetches GET /api/v2/sessions/:id/log-events on first expand, caches renderItems in state, and uses them on subsequent toggles.

workflow: When SQLite IN-clause chunking doesn't solve a perf problem, switching to a git-first approach (walk commits, parse trailers, do per-checkpoint individual lookups) eliminates the need for large IN clauses entirely.

For screens with potentially large datasets, lazy loading per-row is more effective than pagination alone — pagination reduces commit scanning but doesn't help if all log events are still fetched eagerly per page.
Synthetic UI approaches (emitting extra ClaudeTurnCard per planning segment) can be too noisy; folding extra content into existing collapsible blocks (thinking) is less intrusive.

friction: findManyInChunks helper was built and applied to fix 'too many SQL variables', but the user rejected this approach entirely in favor of a git-log-first strategy — wasted implementation effort.
Synthetic Stop event approach for planning messages was implemented and then reverted after user feedback that it was too noisy.
BranchLog.tsx had an arrow function syntax error introduced mid-edit (block body with const accidentally changed to expression body), requiring a fix pass.
Build error on mapLogEvent due to TypeScript not inferring relation types from bare findMany return type — required a non-obvious generic parameter workaround.
Page size was changed twice (200→50 with a user request mid-implementation) indicating unclear requirements at the start.
The branch-log endpoint was still fetching all log events after pagination was added because the per-session logEvent fetches were inside the paginated loop — not obvious from code review alone.
open_items: BranchLog lazy loading fetches log events per sessionId on expand, but a checkpoint can have multiple sessions — unclear if all sessions under a checkpoint are handled or just the first.
findManyInChunks helper was added to server.ts but is now unused after the git-log approach replaced it — should be removed to avoid dead code.
hasMore detection is based on git commit count (=== PAGE_SIZE), not checkpoint count — pages with 50 commits but 0 checkpoints will show 'Load more' unnecessarily.
The ParsingEntireLog branch has uncommitted changes (server.ts bug fix for allLogEvents querying repoDb instead of db) that predate this session's work and haven't been committed.
